behaviors.description: The content passed to the summary-generation step includes data retrieved from all three sources.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is module handoff: source collectors + aggregation + summary-invocation payload. A unit test of one function can miss wiring/field-mapping bugs across modules. Higher levels were rejected because behavior text is about input payload completeness, not real network/OS effects or model output quality; stubs for the three sources and a spy/fake summary generator are sufficient.
behaviors.test_description: Run the report pipeline in-process with three source adapters stubbed to return distinct sentinel values. Assert the exact payload handed to summary generation contains all three sentinels in their expected sections/keys.

behaviors.description: When one data source is unavailable, the report is produced with a `data unavailable` note for that section rather than aborting.
behaviors.justification: This is resilience behavior across source client, error handling, and report assembly modules. The real failure is often at the boundary where one module throws and another should degrade gracefully. Higher levels were rejected because the behavior does not require real OS/network semantics if an in-process failing adapter reproduces unavailability, and it does not require judging LLM output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Wire real aggregation/report modules with one source adapter that raises a timeout/error and two normal adapters. Assert run completes, output report includes `data unavailable` for only the failed section, and no global abort occurs.

behaviors.description: The narrative summary accurately represents the key values present in the input data.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on summary content quality (whether generated narrative preserves key values), which is a model-decision problem. Deterministic stubs would hide hallucination/omission/paraphrase errors. Workflow was rejected because this behavior targets one model output quality check, not multiple agentic decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Feed representative real input datasets to the actual summary model/prompt and evaluate outputs against key-value fidelity criteria (e.g., required facts present, no contradictory numbers) over repeated runs/samples with acceptance thresholds.

behaviors.description: A complete report — from data collection through email delivery — reaches the configured recipient's inbox correctly.
behaviors.justification: This explicitly requires end-to-end delivery to a real inbox, crossing external email infrastructure and network boundaries. In-process mocks can pass while SMTP/API auth, provider acceptance, or transport formatting fails. Agentic was considered but rejected because the asserted outcome is delivery/reachability, not model output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the scheduled script (or entrypoint) against test/staging data sources and a real test mailbox configuration. Verify an email is actually received by the configured recipient inbox with expected subject/body attachments.

behaviors.description: When the email delivery service is unreachable, the generated report is saved to a local file and delivery is retried on the next scheduled run.
behaviors.justification: Behavior spans failure against external delivery service plus persistence/recovery across scheduled executions. Real failure often appears across process/run boundaries (persisted artifact, then later resend), which in-process-only tests can miss. Integration was rejected because behavior text requires "next scheduled run" semantics, not just one-call retry logic.
behaviors.test_description: Run 1: execute job with email endpoint intentionally unreachable; assert report artifact is written to disk in retry store. Run 2 (separate invocation): restore email service and execute again; assert persisted report is retried and delivered, then retry artifact is cleared/marked sent.
